mainTopic: ייאוש בגזלן וגנב · שטף נהר קוריו · התורם שלא מדעת
overview: (1) The Gemara analyzes the Mishnah's comparison of a robber and a thief regarding ייאוש, clarifying that a thief usually does not see the theft, so Rav Papa explains it refers to armed robbers. (2) A Mishnah states that items swept by a river into another's field belong to the finder due to ייאוש, and the Gemara clarifies the distinction between cases where the owner can save them easily versus with difficulty. (3) The Gemara discusses תרומה taken without permission, analyzing whether the owner's subsequent approval (כלך אצל יפות) validates the תרומה retroactively, and Rav Acha bar Rav Ashi's ruling on eating dates given by a sharecropper. (4) The Gemara analyzes a case where the owner rejoices while dew is still on the produce, discussing whether this constitutes valid ייאוש based on the verse 'כי יותן'. — discussion continues into next amud.

sugyaBoundaries.gist: The Gemara analyzes the Mishnah's comparison of a robber and a thief regarding ייאוש, clarifying that a thief usually does not see the theft, so Rav Papa explains it refers to armed robbers.

A Mishnah states that items swept by a river into another's field belong to the finder due to ייאוש, and the Gemara clarifies the distinction between cases where the owner can save them easily versus with difficulty.

The Gemara discusses תרומה taken without permission, analyzing whether the owner's subsequent approval (כלך אצל יפות) validates the תרומה retroactively, and Rav Acha bar Rav Ashi's ruling on eating dates given by a sharecropper.

The Gemara analyzes a case where the owner rejoices while dew is still on the produce, discussing whether this constitutes valid ייאוש based on the verse 'כי יותן'. — discussion continues into next amud.

steps.title: The Mishnah's ruling on ייאוש in cases of גזלן, גנב, and ירדן
steps.stepSummary: The Mishnah rules that in cases of a robber, a thief, or a river taking an item, the owner's ייאוש transfers ownership, so the recipient keeps it.
steps.whatsHappening: The Mishnah establishes that ייאוש transfers ownership in three cases: גזלן, גנב, and ירדן. This is the opening statement of the sugya.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The Mishnah groups a river with human thieves, implying the owner sees the loss in all three. This visibility allows ייאוש to effect קניין.

keyTerms.meaning: The Jordan River, representing any river that takes property.
A robber who takes property openly.
A thief who takes property secretly.
Despair of recovery, enabling ownership transfer.
Acquisition of ownership.

steps.whatToRemember: The Mishnah treats גנב and גזלן identically regarding ייאוש, implying the owner sees the taking in both cases.
steps.confusionAlert: A גנב typically acts secretly, so how can the owner see the theft to despair? This is the crux of the Gemara's challenge.
steps.whyThisMatters: This establishes the baseline halacha that ייאוש can transfer ownership even in cases of theft or natural force, provided the owner is aware.

phrases.aramaic: וְכֵן יַרְדֵּן שֶׁנָּטַל מִזֶּה וְנָתַן לָזֶה,
phrases.english: And likewise, the Jordan that took from this and gave to that,
What was taken — he took,
And what was given — he gave.

steps.triggerLanguage: וְכֵן יַרְדֵּן שֶׁנָּטַל מִזֶּה וְנָתַן לָזֶה

rashi.hebrew: ירדן - וכן שאר נהרות ותנא זה על יד הירדן היה יושב:
rashi.english: The Jordan River - And this is the case for other rivers. This sage (teaching the Baraita) was sitting by the Jordan River.
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the Mishnah's mention of ירדן is a specific example of a general rule that applies to all rivers, and the sage teaching this law lived near the Jordan River.

tosafot.hebrew: מה שנתן נתן - אתיא כר' דאמר בפרק הגוזל [בתרא] (ב"ק דף קיד.) סתם גנב וגזלן הוי יאוש בעלים:
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות explains that the Mishnah's ruling that the thief or robber keeps what they took and gave away relies on the opinion of רבי, who holds that there is ייאוש in cases of גנב and גזלן.

steps.title: Challenge: How does the owner see a גנב to despair?
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara challenges the Mishnah's inclusion of a גנב, asking how the owner can see the theft to despair if a thief acts secretly.
steps.whatsHappening: The Gemara challenges the Mishnah's inclusion of גנב. It questions how ייאוש applies when the theft is secret.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The Gemara concedes that גזלן and ירדן are visible, allowing ייאוש. However, a גנב acts in secret. If unseen, the owner cannot despair, so no קניין occurs.

keyTerms.meaning: Granted / It is clear that (conceding one part of a difficulty).
That he sees him (the owner sees the thief/robber).
He despairs (of recovering the item).

steps.whatToRemember: The difficulty hinges on the definition of גנב as someone who steals secretly, making ייאוש impossible.
steps.confusionAlert: The Gemara isn't questioning the halacha of ייאוש itself, but rather the factual premise that a גנב is seen.
steps.whyThisMatters: This forces a redefinition of the term גנב in the Mishnah to resolve the logical contradiction.

phrases.aramaic: בִּשְׁלָמָא גַּזְלָן וְיַרְדֵּן, דְּקָא חָזֵי לְהוּ וּמִיָּאַשׁ.
phrases.english: In the case of a robber and the Jordan, he sees them and despairs.

phrases.aramaic: אֶלָּא גַּנָּב, מִי קָא חָזֵי לֵיהּ דְּמִיָּאַשׁ?
phrases.english: But a thief, does he see him that he despairs?
Rav Pappa interprets it: armed bandits.
phrases.notes: Refers to the term 'thief' in the baraita, clarifying it means armed bandits whose taking is visible.

phrases.english: If so, this is a robber!
Two types of robber.

steps.triggerLanguage: בִּשְׁלָמָא גַּזְלָן וְיַרְדֵּן, דְּקָא חָזֵי לְהוּ וּמִיָּאַשׁ. אֶלָּא גַּנָּב, מִי קָא חָזֵי לֵיהּ דְּמִיָּאַשׁ?

rashi.hebrew: בלסטים מזוין - ובחזקה נטלה הימנו ומעיקרא ידע ומייאש:
rashi.english: Armed robber - He took it from him by force, and from the outset, the owner knew and despaired (of recovering it).
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that לִסְטִים מְזוּיּוֹן refers to armed robbers who take by force, meaning the owner witnesses the act from the outset and therefore יָאֵשׁ immediately.

steps.title: Baraita on items swept by a river
steps.stepSummary: A baraita states that items swept by a river into another's field belong to the finder due to the owner's ייאוש, and the Gemara infers that without explicit ייאוש, the items do not belong to the finder.
steps.whatsHappening: The Gemara introduces a baraita about items swept by a river. It infers that acquisition requires explicit ייאוש.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The baraita explicitly attributes acquisition to the owner's ייאוש. The Gemara infers that if ייאוש must be stated, then in unstated cases, the finder does not acquire.

keyTerms.meaning: A river swept away his beams.
These belong to him (the finder).
Because the owners despaired.
The reason is that the owners despaired.
But in an unspecified case, no.

steps.whatToRemember: The baraita explicitly links acquisition to ייאוש, implying that without it, there is no acquisition.
steps.confusionAlert: The inference here is a standard Gemara move: explicit mention implies exclusion of the unstated case.
steps.whyThisMatters: This establishes the baseline challenge: does ייאוש need to be conscious or explicit?

phrases.aramaic: תָּא שְׁמַע: שָׁטַף נָהָר קוֹרָיו, עֵצָיו, וַאֲבָנָיו,
phrases.english: Come and hear: A river swept away his beams, his wood, and his stones,
and placed them into the field of his fellow –
these are his,
because the owners despaired.

phrases.aramaic: טַעְמָא דְּנִתְיָאֲשׁוּ הַבְּעָלִים, הָא סְתָמָא – לָא!
phrases.english: The reason is that the owners despaired, but in an unspecified case – no!
With what are we dealing here? When he is able to rescue.

steps.triggerLanguage: תא שמע: שטף נהר קוריו, עצייו, ואבניו, ונתנו בתוך שדה חברו – הרי אלו שלו, מפני שניתיאשו הבעלים. טעמא דניתיאשו הבעלים, הא סתמא – לא!

rashi.hebrew: הרי אלו כו' - ה"ג הרי אלו שלו מפני שנתיאשו הבעלים דכל שטיפת נהר כקורות עצים ואבנים הבעלים ידעו בה מיד דיש לה קול:
rashi.english: They are his, etc. - The correct text is: 'They are his, because the owners have despaired.' For anything swept away by a river, like beams of wood and stones, the owners immediately become aware of it because it is something that creates a commotion (is widely known)."
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the baraita's mention of ייאוש is necessary because items like beams and stones swept by a river create a commotion, ensuring the owner immediately knows of the loss and thus despaired.

rashi.hebrew: הא סתמא - הא אם היתה אבידה אחרת שאין לנו לומר שידעו הבעלים לא הויא של מוצאה קשיא לרבא:
rashi.english: This is the default case - But if it were another type of lost item, where we cannot assume that the owners were aware [of the loss], it would not belong to the finder. This presents a difficulty for Rava.
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that the Gemara's inference (הא סתמא לא) challenges רבא's view by suggesting that in cases of other lost items where the owner's awareness and subsequent ייאוש cannot be assumed, the finder does not acquire the item.

rashi.hebrew: כשיכול להציל - ודכוותה במציאה אחרת דבר שיש בו סימן דיכול ליתן סימן וליטול מודינא בה דחייב להחזיר:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the resolution 'כשיכול להציל' applies equally to other lost items with a סימן, where the owner can identify and reclaim them, meaning the owner does not despair and the finder is obligated to return the item.

tosafot.hebrew: שטף נהר קוריו עציו ואבניו - ה"ג אם נתיאשו הבעלים הרי אלו שלו וצ"ל דמיירי ביכול להציל בקל אם רודף בשעת שטיפה דאי לאו הכי הוה כזוטו של ים שאבוד ממנו ומכל אדם ועל זה לא היה אומר הא סתם לא אלא איירי ביכול להציל בקל בשעת שטיפה ואפילו אין בהן סימן ולפי שיעכבן עקולי ופשורי ויקחם שם ואפי' יקדמו אחרים ויקחו יחזירום לו לפי שניכר שהוא בעליו לפי שרדף מיד וס"ד דמיירי דאם לא רדף מיד בשעת שטיפה שלא יכול עוד כלל להציל כדמוכח בסיפא לפי שיקחום אחרים כשיעכבום עקולי ופשורי ולא יחזירו לפי שאין בהן סימן וה"פ אם נתיאשו הבעלים דהיינו שהיו שם בשעת שטיפה ולא רדפו ואפילו אומרים שאין מתיאשים אין בכך כלום דודאי הן מתיאשים בלבן הא סתמא שלא היו בעלים בשעת שטיפה ולא נתיאשו עד שידעו הבעלים ששטפה נהר ואז כבר אינו יכול להציל לפי שאין בו סימן לא דכשמצאן באיסורא אתא לידיה שהבעלים לא ידעו עדיין אלמא יאוש שלא מדעת לא הוי יאוש ומשני ביכול להציל אפי' אחר שטיפה כגון שיש באבידה סימן שהבעלים אינן מתיאשין כשידעו ופריך אי הכי אימא סיפא ואם היו הבעלים מרדפין כו' מאי אריא מרדפין אפילו היו בעלים שם בשעת שטיפה ולא רדפו נמי יחזיר ומשני ביכול להציל אחר שטיפה ע"י הדחק דאע"פ שיש בו סימן בטורח יכול להציל לפי שיתרחקו הרבה שהנהר מוליכן ברחוק ולכך אם היה בשעת שטיפה ואינו מרדף ודאי מתיאש כיון שעתה יכול להציל בלא דוחק ואינו מציל אבל אין הבעלים בשעת שטיפה יחזיר דכשידעו הבעלים לא יתיאשו ויצילו על ידי הדחק וא"ת מנלן הא סתמא חייב להחזיר דלמא נתיאשו אתא לאפוקי שהיו שם ורדפו דחייב להחזיר וי"ל דא"כ לא ליתני אם נתיאשו אלא לתני סתמא שטף נהר קוריו עציו ואבניו הרי אלו שלו דפשיטא דלא מיירי ברדפו בשעת שטיפה דהא קתני סיפא דאם היו הבעלים מרדפים חייב ואין להקשות דמצי דייק מסיפא טעמא דמרדפין הא סתמא הרי אלו שלו דלעולם נימא דהוא הדין סתמא דיחזיר ולא נקט מרדפין אלא לאפוקי שהיו הבעלים בשעת שטיפה ולא רדפו וע"י סיפא ידעינן דמיירי באין יכול להציל אבל רש"י דגרס מפני שנתיאשו הבעלים משמע לישנא דפשיט ליה דנתיאשו א"כ מרישא גופיה מצי לאוכוחי דמיירי באין יכול להציל ולא היה צריך להביא סיפא ותו מנלן דהא סתמא לא דאפילו סתמא נמי מצי מיירי:
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות explains that the baraita must refer to a case where the owner could easily rescue the items during the flood, otherwise they would be considered lost forever like the 'little finger of the sea' and the inference from ייאוש would not apply.

meforshim.interplaySummary: While רש״י focuses on the owner's immediate awareness due to the commotion of the items, תוספות emphasizes the owner's physical ability to rescue the items during the flood as the prerequisite for the baraita's logic regarding ייאוש.

steps.title: Challenge from the latter clause of the baraita
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara challenges the resolution by pointing to the latter clause of the baraita, which states that if the owners pursue the items, the finder must return them, implying that without pursuit, they do not need to return them even if rescue was possible.
steps.whatsHappening: The Gemara raises a difficulty from the second part of the same baraita. It questions the distinction between pursuing and not pursuing.
steps.deeperAnalysis: If rescue is easy, the owner's failure to rescue should indicate ייאוש regardless of pursuit. The distinction between pursuing and not pursuing seems unnecessary.

keyTerms.meaning: If so, say the latter clause.
If the owners were pursuing them.
He is obligated to return.
If they are able to rescue.
Why does it mention pursuing?
Even if they are not pursuing.

steps.whatToRemember: The latter clause distinguishes between pursuing and not pursuing, which seems redundant if rescue is easy.
steps.confusionAlert: The challenge hinges on the assumption that easy rescue makes pursuit irrelevant.
steps.whyThisMatters: This forces a refinement of the distinction between easy and difficult rescue.

phrases.english: If so, say the latter clause:

phrases.aramaic: אִם הָיוּ הַבְּעָלִים מְרַדְּפִין אַחֲרֵיהֶם – חַיָּיב לְהַחְזִיר.
phrases.english: If the owners were pursuing after them – he is obligated to return.

phrases.aramaic: אִי בִּיכוֹלִין לְהַצִּיל, מַאי אִרְיָא מְרַדְּפִין?
phrases.english: If they are able to rescue, what need of pursuing?
Even if they are not pursuing also!
With what are we dealing here?
In being able to rescue by means of difficulty.
Pursuing – not despair,
Not pursuing – despair, he despaired.

steps.triggerLanguage: אי הכי אימא סיפא: אם היו הבעלים מרדפין אחריהם – חייב להחזיר. אי ביכולין להציל, מאי איירי מרדפין? אפילו אין מרדפין נמי!

rashi.hebrew: אפילו אין מרדפין נמי - דסמכי אהצלה דלמחר וליומא אוחרא ולא מייאשי:
rashi.english: Even if they are not chasing [after it], as well - because he relies on rescuing it tomorrow or the day after, and he does not despair [of recovering it].
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that if the owners are not pursuing the item, they still do not have ייאוש because they rely on being able to recover it the next day.

rashi.hebrew: ע"י הדחק - ואם לא ימהר להציל לא יצילו הלכך אין מרדפין אפקורי אפקרינהו דהא ידעו ולא הצילו:
rashi.english: Under pressure - And if they do not act quickly to rescue [the item], it will not be saved. Therefore, if they do not pursue it, as they have declared it ownerless, since they knew [of the loss] and did not save it.
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that the case of 'under pressure' means the item will be lost if not rescued immediately, so failure to pursue in such a case constitutes ייאוש.

steps.title: Baraita on תרומה taken without permission
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara introduces a baraita discussing תרומה separated without the owner's consent, stating that if the owner is not concerned about it being גזל, the תרומה is valid, and explains how to determine the owner's attitude through his reaction.
steps.whatsHappening: The Gemara cites a baraita about תרומה taken without permission. It establishes that validity depends on whether the owner considers it גזל.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The baraita introduces a test for owner approval: 'כלך אצל יפות'. If better produce exists, the תרומה is valid. This sets the stage for the debate on timing.

keyTerms.meaning: One who separates teruma without the owner's knowledge.
Robbery/theft; if the owner views the act as theft, the teruma is invalid.
Go to the better [produce]; the owner's statement used to test his approval.

steps.whatToRemember: The baraita's test for owner approval: 'כלך אצל יפות' + better produce found = valid תרומה.
steps.confusionAlert: The phrase 'כלך אצל יפות' is ambiguous; it could be sincere approval or sarcastic anger. The baraita uses the existence of better produce to disambiguate.
steps.whyThisMatters: This establishes the principle that owner approval can be inferred from behavior, but the timing of that approval relative to the act of תרומה is the crux of the subsequent debate.

phrases.english: Come and hear:

phrases.aramaic: כֵּיצַד אָמְרוּ הַתּוֹרֵם שֶׁלֹּא מִדַּעַת תְּרוּמָתוֹ תְּרוּמָה –
phrases.english: How did they say: One who separates teruma without knowledge, his teruma is teruma –

phrases.aramaic: הֲרֵי שֶׁיָּרַד לְתוֹךְ שְׂדֵה חֲבֵירוֹ וְלִיקֵּט
phrases.english: Behold, one who descended into his fellow's field and gathered
and separated teruma without permission,
If he is concerned on account of robbery –
his teruma is not teruma,
and if not – his teruma is teruma.

phrases.aramaic: וּמִנַּיִן הוּא יוֹדֵעַ אִם חוֹשֵׁשׁ מִשּׁוּם
phrases.english: And from where does he know if he is concerned on account of
robbery or not?
Behold, the owner came and found him,
and said to him: 'You should have gone to the good ones.'
If better ones than them were found –
his teruma is teruma, and if not –
his teruma is not teruma.
The owners gathered and added to them,

phrases.aramaic: בֵּין כָּךְ וּבֵין כָּךְ – תְּרוּמָתוֹ תְּרוּמָה.
phrases.english: either way – his teruma is teruma.

steps.triggerLanguage: תָּא שְׁמַע: כֵּיצַד אָמְרוּ הַתּוֹרֵם שֶׁלֹּא מִדַּעַת

rashi.hebrew: שלא מדעת - בעלים:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that the phrase שלא מדעת refers specifically to the owner's lack of awareness.

rashi.hebrew: וליקט - לצורך בעל הבית:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the act of picking (וליקט) was done for the benefit of the owner.

rashi.hebrew: ואם חושש - בעל הבית ומקפיד על מה שעשה זה:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י defines חושש משום גזל as the owner being concerned and objecting to the unauthorized action.

rashi.hebrew: משום גזל - שתרם תרומתו בלא רשותו:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י specifies that the concern of גזל stems from the תרומה being separated without the owner's permission.

rashi.hebrew: כלך אצל יפות - היה לך לילך אצל יפות לתת מהן לכהן:
rashi.english: Go to the better ones— you should have gone to the better ones to give from them to the priest.
rashi.takeaway: רש״י interprets the owner's statement כלך אצל יפות as a directive that the separator should have selected from the better produce to give to the כהן.

tosafot.hebrew: אם יש יפות מהן תרומתו תרומה - דמסתמא גם מתחילה היה דעתו כן וא"ת ונימא דהשתא ודאי ניחא ליה ביפות אבל מתחילה בשעה שתרם אי הוה ידע לא הוה ניחא ליה דאין דרך לתרום מן היפות כדאמרינן לעיל (בבא מציעא דף כא:) גבי דבר שיש בו סימן דאפילו לרבא חייב להכריז דאע"ג דהשתא מתיאש אין בכך כלום וי"ל דשאני הכא דכיון דחזינן ביה דניחא ליה השתא אמרינן נמי ניחא ליה מעיקרא משום מצוה:
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות addresses the concern that the owner might only approve of the better produce now but not initially, resolving it by stating that since the owner is pleased now, we assume he was also pleased initially due to the merit of the מצוה.

steps.title: Challenge: How can retroactive approval validate תרומה?
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara challenges the baraita's ruling that תרומה is valid if better produce is found, arguing that at the time of separation, the owner had not yet approved, so the act should be invalid.
steps.whatsHappening: The Gemara challenges the baraita's logic. It questions how later approval can validate an earlier act.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The baraita says the תרומה is valid based on a later test. The Gemara presses: at the time of the act, the owner hadn't approved. If approval is necessary, it must exist then.

keyTerms.meaning: At the time that he separated [teruma].
He did not yet know [of the owner's approval].

steps.whatToRemember: The challenge hinges on the timing: approval must exist at the time of the act, not after.
steps.confusionAlert: The Gemara is not questioning the baraita's facts, but its legal logic: can a later event validate an earlier act?
steps.whyThisMatters: This sets up the need for a resolution that explains how the owner's later reaction can relate to the earlier act.

phrases.aramaic: וְכִי נִמְצְאוּ יָפוֹת מֵהֶן תְּרוּמָתוֹ תְּרוּמָה, אַמַּאי?
phrases.english: And if better ones were found, his teruma is teruma — why?

phrases.aramaic: בְּעִידָּנָא דִּתְרַם הָא לָא הֲוָה יָדַע!
phrases.english: At the time that he separated, he did not know!

phrases.aramaic: תַּרְגְּמַהּ רָבָא אַלִּיבָּא דְּאַבָּיֵי: דְּשַׁוְּיֵהּ שָׁלִיחַ.
phrases.english: Rava explained it according to Abaye: That the owner designated him as an agent.

steps.triggerLanguage: וְכִי נִמְצְאוּ יָפוֹת מֵהֶן תְּרוּמָתוֹ תְּרוּמָה, אַמַּאי?

rashi.hebrew: לא הוה ידע - אלמא כיון דלכי ידע דניחא ליה אמרינן מעיקרא נמי ניחא ליה ולענין יאוש נמי כיון דלכי ידע מייאש מעיקרא נמי הוי יאוש:
rashi.english: He did not know—thus, since when he finds out, it is acceptable to him, we say that retroactively it was also acceptable to him. And regarding the matter of despair , since when he finds out he would despair, retroactively it is also considered despair.
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the Gemara's challenge implies a principle of retroactive intent: if the owner is pleased upon discovering the produce was better, we consider him pleased from the outset, and similarly, if he would despair upon discovery, we consider יאוש to have occurred retroactively.

steps.title: Proof from Scripture: Agency is required for תרומה
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara supports Rava's resolution by citing a verse that includes agents in the commandment of תרומה, proving that תרומה must be done with the owner's knowledge via agency.
steps.whatsHappening: The Gemara brings a ראיה to support Rava's resolution. It cites a verse to include agents in the mitzvah.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The verse 'אתם גם אתם' includes agents. The logic is: if the owner must act with knowledge, the agent must also act with the owner's knowledge.

keyTerms.meaning: So too it is reasonable [to understand].
To include your agents.
With your knowledge/consent.

steps.whatToRemember: The verse 'אתם גם אתם' includes agents, but requires the owner's knowledge.
steps.confusionAlert: The verse is used to prove that תרומה cannot be done without the owner's knowledge, hence the need for agency.
steps.whyThisMatters: This establishes a scriptural basis for the requirement of agency in תרומה, reinforcing Rava's interpretation.

phrases.english: So too, it is reasonable,

phrases.aramaic: דְּאִי סָלְקָא דַעְתָּךְ דְּלָא שַׁוְּויֵהּ שָׁלִיחַ,
phrases.english: for if it enters your mind that he did not designate him as an agent,
would his teruma be teruma?

phrases.aramaic: וְהָא ״אַתֶּם״ ״גַּם אַתֶּם״ אָמַר רַחֲמָנָא לְרַבּוֹת שְׁלוּחֲכֶם.
phrases.english: And yet the Merciful One said 'you' 'also you' to include your agents.

phrases.aramaic: מָה אַתֶּם לְדַעְתְּכֶם, אַף שְׁלוּחֲכֶם לְדַעְתְּכֶם.
phrases.english: What are you, by your knowledge, so too your agents, by your knowledge.

steps.triggerLanguage: הָכִי נָמֵי מִסְתַּבְּרָא, דְּאִי סָלְקָא דַעְתָּךְ דְּלָא שַׁוְּויֵהּ שָׁלִיחַ

rashi.hebrew: אתם גם אתם - כן תרימו גם אתם מהכא נפקא לן שלוחו של אדם כמותו לתרומה שהשליח שתרם תרומתו תרומה וכיון דשליחות מהכא נפקא לן על כרחך שלוחכם דומיא דאתם בעינן:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the verse 'אתם גם אתם' teaches that an agent's תרומה is valid, and since this source establishes agency, the agent must be analogous to the owner and thus require the owner's knowledge.

rashi.hebrew: אף שלוחכם לדעתכם - שהבעלים מינוהו שליח:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that 'לדעתכם' means the owner must have appointed the person as an agent.

tosafot.hebrew: הכי גרסינן ה"נ מסתברא דאי לא שוייה שליח ולא גרסינן ותסברא:
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות corrects the text to read 'הכי נמי מסתברא דאי לא שוייה שליח' instead of 'ותסברא'.

steps.title: Refining the case: Agent appointed but not specified which produce
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara clarifies the exact scenario: the owner appointed the person as an agent to separate תרומה, but did not specify which produce, so the agent separated from better produce, and the owner's reaction determines validity.
steps.whatsHappening: The Gemara refines the resolution. It specifies that the owner appointed an agent but did not specify which produce.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The owner appointed a שליח but did not specify the produce. The agent separated from better produce. The owner's reaction is tested by whether even better produce exists.

keyTerms.meaning: Rather, with what are we dealing here?
He did not say to him: 'Separate teruma from these'.
From the intermediate [quality produce].

steps.whatToRemember: The agent was appointed but not given specific instructions on which produce to separate from.
steps.confusionAlert: The distinction between appointing an agent and specifying the produce is crucial here.
steps.whyThisMatters: This clarifies how agency and the test of 'better produce' interact in the baraita.

phrases.english: Rather, with what are we dealing here?
Such as where he designated him as an agent,
and said to him: "Go and separate teruma",
and he did not say to him "separate teruma from these".

phrases.aramaic: וּסְתָמֵיהּ דְּבַעַל הַבַּיִת כִּי תָּרֵים מִבֵּינוֹנִית (הוּא) תָּרֵים,
phrases.english: And the owner's unspecified intent is that when he separates, he separates from the intermediate quality,
and he went and separated from the better quality,

phrases.aramaic: וּבָא בַּעַל הַבַּיִת וּמְצָאוֹ וַאֲמַר לֵיהּ:
phrases.english: and the owner came and found him and said to him:
"You should have gone to the better quality",
if better quality than them is found –
his teruma is teruma, and if not –
his teruma is not teruma.

steps.triggerLanguage: אֶלָּא הָכָא בְּמַאי עָסְקִינַן – כְּגוֹן דְּשַׁוְּיֵהּ שָׁלִיחַ

tosafot.hebrew: ואם לאו אין תרומתו תרומה - וא"ת דבפרק אלמנה ניזונית (כתובות דף צט: ושם ד"ה פיחת) אמרינן דסתמיה דבעל הבית הוי אחד מחמשים ואם השליח פיחת י' או הוסיף י' תרומתו תרומה דמצי א"ל בהכי אמדתיך ה"נ נימא הכי וי"ל דהתם הוי כולה שיעור תרומה ויש שתורם כך ויש שתורם כך לכך מצי א"ל כיון שלא פירשת לי בהכי אמדתיך אבל מיפות אין רגילות לתרום ולכך לא היה לו לתרום בשום ענין מהם בלא רשותו:
tosafot.english: And if not, his separation of terumah is not valid – And if you say, in the chapter (Ketubot 99b ) we say that the default for a homeowner is one-fiftieth, and if the agent reduced or increased by ten [parts], his separation of terumah is valid because the homeowner can say, ‘This is how I estimated you’. Here too, let us say the same. And it can be answered that there it is all within the range of the measure of terumah, as some separate this way and some separate that way, and therefore he can say, ‘Since you did not specify for me, this is how I estimated you.’ But regarding mif'ot [an extraordinary or unconventional action], it is not customary to separate terumah in that way, and therefore he should not have separated in any such manner without the owner’s permission.
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות explains that the agent's separation from better produce is invalid because it falls outside the customary range of תרומה measures, unlike the case in כתובות where variations within the standard measure are permitted.

steps.title: Incident at מָרִי בַּר אִסָּק's Orchard
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara recounts an incident where Ameimar and Rav Ashi ate fruit given by a sharecropper, while Mar Zutra refrained, and the owner's reaction was ambiguous.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא shifts to a narrative incident involving אַמֵּימָר, מַר זוּטְרָא, and רַב אַשֵּׁי at an orchard. The sharecropper brings fruit, and the owner's ambiguous reaction sets up a practical test of the baraita's rules.
steps.deeperAnalysis: This story parallels the baraita's scenario where a third party acts without explicit permission. The owner's question about better fruit mirrors the phrase 'כלך אצל יפות', creating ambiguity about whether this constitutes approval or sarcasm.

keyTerms.meaning: They happened to come to the orchard
His sharecropper
From those better [fruits]

phrases.aramaic: אַמֵּימָר וּמָר זוּטְרָא וְרַב אָשֵׁי אִקְּלַעוּ
phrases.english: Ameimar and Mar Zutra and Rav Ashi happened to come
to the orchard of Mari bar Isak,

phrases.aramaic: אַיְיתִי אֲרִיסֵיהּ תַּמְרֵי וְרִימּוֹנֵי וּשְׁדָא קַמַּיְיהוּ.
phrases.english: his sharecropper brought dates and pomegranates and threw before them.

phrases.aramaic: אַמֵּימָר וְרַב אָשֵׁי אָכְלִי, מָר זוּטְרָא לָא אֲכַל.
phrases.english: Ameimar and Rav Ashi ate, Mar Zutra did not eat.
Meanwhile Mari bar Isak came,
found them, and said to his sharecropper:

phrases.aramaic: אַמַּאי לָא אַיְיתֵית לְהוּ לְרַבָּנַן מֵהָנָךְ שַׁפִּירָתָא?
phrases.english: Why did you not bring to the Sages from those good ones?

steps.triggerLanguage: אַמֵּימָר וּמָר זוּטְרָא וְרַב אָשֵׁי אִקְּלַעוּ לְבוּסְתָּנָא דְּמָרִי בַּר אִיסַק

rashi.hebrew: לא אכל - דגזל נינהו שהבעלים לא ידעו:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that מר זוטרא did not eat the fruit because it constituted גזל, as the owner was unaware of the sharecropper giving the fruit.

tosafot.hebrew: מר זוטרא לא אכל - וא"ת ואמאי לא אכל האמר רבא פרק הגוזל בתרא (ב"ק דף קיט.) אריסא מדנפשיה קא זבן ויש לומר דהתם שהביא האריס מבית דמסתמא ממה שהגיע לחלקו מביא אבל הכא שהביא מן הפרדס היה חושש מר זוטרא שמא בשעת חלוקה לא יאמר לבעל הפרדס תטול כנגד מה שנתתי להם ורב אשי לא היה חושש לזה ולכך אכל קודם שבא מרי בר איסק משום דאריס מדנפשיה קא יהיב דאין לומר שהיה סומך שיתרצה מרי בר איסק כשידע דהלכה כאביי ואע"ג דהשתא ניחא ליה מעיקרא לא הוה ניחא ליה:
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות addresses why מר זוטרא did not eat despite רבא's ruling that a sharecropper sells from his own portion, explaining that רבא's ruling applies when the sharecropper brings fruit from his home, but here the fruit was taken directly from the orchard, raising concern that the owner would deduct the value from the sharecropper's share during division, whereas רב אשי did not have this concern and ate before the owner arrived.

meforshim.interplaySummary: רש״י simply states that מר זוטרא refrained from eating due to גזל, while תוספות provides a nuanced halachic distinction explaining why מר זוטרא was concerned about the owner deducting the value from the sharecropper's portion, a concern רב אשי did not share.

steps.title: Challenge to מַר זוּטְרָא's Caution
steps.stepSummary: Ameimar and Rav Ashi challenge Mar Zutra, asking why he didn't eat the fruit, citing the baraita that says if better produce is found, the act is valid.
steps.whatsHappening: אַמֵּימָר and רַב אַשֵּׁי raise a קשיא against מַר זוּטְרָא's refusal to eat. They argue that the owner's question implies better fruit exists, validating the sharecropper's act according to the baraita.
steps.deeperAnalysis: This applies the baraita directly to the incident. The challenge assumes the owner's words function like 'כלך אצל יפות', meaning the act should be valid and מַר זוּטְרָא should have eaten.

keyTerms.meaning: Now why is the Master not eating?
But isn't it taught [in a baraita]?

phrases.aramaic: אֲמַרוּ לֵיהּ אַמֵּימָר וְרַב אָשֵׁי לְמָר זוּטְרָא:
phrases.english: Ameimar and Rav Ashi said to Mar Zutra:
Now why is the Master not eating?

phrases.aramaic: וְהָתַנְיָא: אִם נִמְצְאוּ יָפוֹת מֵהֶן – תְּרוּמָתוֹ תְּרוּמָה!
phrases.english: And it is taught: If better ones are found from them – his teruma is teruma!
He said to them, thus said Rava:

phrases.aramaic: לֹא אָמְרוּ כְּלָךְ אֵצֶל יָפוֹת אֶלָּא
phrases.english: They did not say at all regarding better ones except
regarding teruma alone,
because it is a mitzva and it is pleasing to him,
But here –
because of shame is that he said thus.

steps.triggerLanguage: אֲמַרוּ לֵיהּ אַמֵּימָר וְרַב אָשֵׁי לְמָר זוּטְרָא: הַשְׁתָּא אַמַּאי לָא אָכֵיל מָר?

rashi.hebrew: אם נמצאו יפות כו' - אלמא גלי דעתיה דניחא ליה ה"נ גלי דעתיה דניחא ליה במה שנתן לנו:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the baraita's case of 'אצל יפות' demonstrates that the owner's words reveal his consent (גלי דעתיה דניחא ליה), and Ameimar and Rav Ashi argue that the owner's question here similarly reveals his consent to the sharecropper's action.

rashi.hebrew: לא אמרו כלך אצל יפות - דהוי גלוי דעת אלא לענין תרומה:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that Mar Zutra's distinction limits the principle of 'גלוי דעת' through 'אצל יפות' to the context of תרומה, where the owner's satisfaction is derived from fulfilling a מצווה, whereas here the owner's words stem from כסופותא (desire for better produce) rather than true consent.

steps.title: Proof from a ברייתא about Dew
steps.stepSummary: The גמרא cites a ברייתא stating that if dew is still on produce and the owner rejoices, it is considered 'כי יותן' (placed), but if it dried, even if he rejoices, it is not.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא introduces a ברייתא to prove that ייאוש must be conscious. The text distinguishes between dew still on produce and dried dew, linking the former to the owner's happiness.
steps.deeperAnalysis: This ראיה supports the view that ייאוש requires awareness at the time of the event. If the owner is happy while dew remains, it counts as 'placed'; if dried, his happiness doesn't count, implying retrospective joy is invalid.

keyTerms.meaning: The dew is still on them
Under the category of 'when it is placed'
They dried

phrases.aramaic: תָּא שְׁמַע: עוֹדֵהוּ הַטַּל עֲלֵיהֶן וְשָׂמַח –
phrases.english: Come and hear: The dew is still upon them and he was glad –
Behold, this is 'when water is placed upon the seed.' If they dried,
Even though he was glad –
They are not 'when water is placed upon the seed.'

steps.triggerLanguage: תָּא שְׁמַע: עוֹדֵהוּ הַטַּל עֲלֵיהֶן וְשָׂמַח – הֲרֵי זֶה בְּכִי יֻתַּן. נִגְּבוּ, אַף עַל פִּי שֶׁשָּׂמַח – אֵינָן בְּכִי יוּתַּן.

rashi.hebrew: עודהו הטל עליהן - המעלה פירותיו לגג וירד עליהן הטל:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the phrase 'עודהו הטל עליהן' refers to a scenario where a person brings his produce to the roof and dew falls upon it.

rashi.hebrew: ושמח - בטל שירד עליהן:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that 'ושמח' means the owner is happy about the dew that fell on the produce.

rashi.hebrew: הרי זה בכי יותן - והוכשרו לטומאה מעתה ועד עולם:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that 'הרי זה בכי יותן' means the produce is susceptible to טומאה from that moment onward.

rashi.hebrew: נגבו - עד שלא מצאן:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that 'נגבו' refers to the dew drying up before the owner finds the produce.

rashi.hebrew: אע"פ ששמח - עכשיו בטל שירד עליהן:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that 'אע
